new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine of fresh grapes not falling under Champagne (2204 10 11), Prosecco (2204 10 93), or other specifically enumerated sparkling wine subheadings (2204 10 19, 2204 10 91), classified under CN subheading 2204 10 94 (Chapter 22). Conforms to subheading note 1 of Chapter 22 regarding excess pressure thresholds.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22041094 is brief, stating only "Sparkling wine – See subheading note 1 to this chapter." The core axiom anchoring this class to CN code 22041094 and its parent SparklingWine is fully captured. Disjointness from Champagne (22041011) and Prosecco-region / Glera-variety wines (sibling subheadings) is represented via complement restrictions on the geographic-origin and grape-variety qualities already present in the TBox. However, the full discriminating criteria for this residual "other" subheading depend on the complete sibling enumeration of all 2204 10 1x and 2204 10 9x subheadings (e.g. 22041019, 22041091), whose specific quality differentiators (e.g. excess-pressure thresholds from subheading note 1, specific PDO/PGI region lists) are not yet modelled for those siblings. Without those sibling axioms, the complement restrictions here are incomplete. Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.55.
